source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
Two half-cells are set up as follows:

Half-Cell A: Strip of Cu(s) in $CuNO_3(aq)$

Half-Cell B: Strip of Zn(s) in $Zn(NO_3)_2(aq)$

When the cells are connected according to the following diagram, the following reaction occurs. What will happen in the salt bridge as the reaction progresses?
source_intake_record.raw_answer_text: The Na+ ions will flow to the Cu/Cu+ half-cell.

choice_map.A: The Na+ ions will flow to the Cu/Cu+ half-cell.
choice_map.B: The Br- ions will flow to the Cu/Cu+ half-cell.
choice_map.C: Electrons will transfer from the Cu/Cu+ half-cell to the Zn/Zn2+ half-cell.
choice_map.D: Electrons will transfer from the Zn/Zn2+ half-cell to the Cu/Cu+ half-cell.

extraction_notes: answer field is numeric index 0 and was mapped to the first choice text
image field contains a PIL image object string, not a path or URL
source_intake_record.subject: Chemistry

issues: metadata.extraction_notes indicates the original image field contained a PIL image object string rather than a path or URL.

image_dependency: question_text explicitly says 'according to the following diagram'
the salt-bridge ion identities referenced in the choices (Na+, Br-) are not stated in the visible text, so the diagram likely supplies essential information
multi_step: solving requires identifying the redox roles of the Cu and Zn half-cells and then inferring ion migration in the salt bridge
the task combines chemistry knowledge about galvanic cells with interpretation of the depicted setup/diagram
there are 4 choices, including distractors about both ion flow and electron flow, indicating more than a one-step lookup
verifiability: the item is effectively multiple-choice with 4 explicit options
answer_text exactly matches one listed choice, enabling straightforward answer checking
the reasoning target is a standard electrochemistry fact pattern, which is generally checkable
